messages.subject: [OGRUG] Quick Survey Until 11 April 2013: Towards a Training Course in Free/Libre/Open Source Licensing and Contracting
messages.subject_clean: [OGRUG] Quick Survey Until 11 April 2013: Towards a Training Course in Free/Libre/Open Source Licensing and Contracting
messages.body_plain:
Hello, My previous email might have had *asterisks* inadvertently
inserted around the URL of the survey, about which I was not aware of.
Please use this link for the survey if sharing with anyone else.
http://freeonlinesurveys.com/s.asp?sid=6pjtq7tro6rrq8y239996

Sorry for the inconvenience.

Joseph Potvin

On Tue, Apr 9, 2013 at 6:29 PM, Joseph Potvin <jpotvin at opman.ca> wrote:
> OGRUG colleagues, Recently I've been contracted by a global firm which is
> very active in the free/libre/open source world, to develop a course on
> minimizing business risk and maximizing business value through
> free/libre/open source licensing and contracting. While the topic itself is
> tangenial to this list, nevertheless I've long considered the R community to
> be amongst the most effective free/libre/open communities. I refer to it
> fairly often as providing something close to an archetype for distributed
> free/libre/open quality control methodology.
>
> My client agrees that the generic contents of the training course we are
> creating should itself be dual-licensed under the GNU-FDL and CC-BY
> licenses.
>
> Responses to this short questionnaire will help us to ensure that the
> generic course meets the needs and preferences of diverse teams and
> individual professionals. This requires 5-to-10 minutes to complete.
>
> http://freeonlinesurveys.com/s.asp?sid=6pjtq7tro6rrq8y239996
> (Note: Some have reported *asterisks* showing up on each end of this URL on
> the receiving end. Please take the URL without them.)
>
> Please suggest improvements to the scope of this work, directly within the
> questionnaire. Report any glitches or typos directly to my email.
>
> The results of this survey will be shared back with recipients (minus the
> list of respondent names and emails) from this location:
> http://www.projectmanagementhotel.com/projects/optimization-free-libre-open
>
> We'll be grateful for any collaboration. Please forward to individuals you
> think would have a direct interest in this.
>
> --
> Joseph Potvin
> Operations Manager | Gestionnaire des operations
> The Opman Company | La compagnie Opman
> http://www.projectmanagementhotel.com/projects/opman-portfolio
> jpotvin at opman.ca
> Mobile: 819-593-5983
> LinkedIn (Google short URL): http://goo.gl/Ssp56
>



-- 
Joseph Potvin
Operations Manager | Gestionnaire des operations
The Opman Company | La compagnie Opman
http://www.projectmanagementhotel.com/projects/opman-portfolio
jpotvin at opman.ca
Mobile: 819-593-5983
LinkedIn (Google short URL): http://goo.gl/Ssp56

messages.subject: [OGRUG] Files mentioned at R meetup April 8
messages.subject_clean: [OGRUG] Files mentioned at R meetup April 8
messages.body_plain:
At the meetup there was some interest in some work I'd done to present 
Sweave, KnitR and odfWeave, as well as Latex -> Epub.

I've put the slides on my server at

http://macnash.telfer.uottawa.ca/~nashjc/visible/

nash-oclug130307.odp -- lightning talk about Latex -> epub.

UOpsych130311.odp -- latest version of a talk about updateable reports 
(including graphics) using Sweave, KnitR or odfWeave.

Best,

John Nash
